villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.2: A good harvest starts with good soil.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.3: I once saw a carrot run away. True story.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.4: My scarecrow has more conversations than me.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.5: I tried crop rotation once. Got dizzy.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.6: My prize pumpkin is more of a prize gourd.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.7: The soil is rich this year.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.8: I wake up before the chickens. Even they think I'm a bit much.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.9: My carrots are the best in town.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.10: Farming is honest work. The soil never lies to you.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.12: The chickens have laid plenty of eggs today.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.13: Could you help me harvest the potatoes, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.14: I talk to my crops. They talk back sometimes.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.15: I tried to grow a diamond once. Got a very shiny rock.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.16: This weather has my crops growing faster than my patience wears thin.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.17: Have you tried my fresh produce, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.18: The sheep found a hole in the fence again. Clever little escape artists.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.19: The wheat is almost ready to harvest.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.20: Nothing beats the smell of fresh soil.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.21: This rain is exactly what my crops needed. Let it pour.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.22: I hope the animals are safe in the barn tonight.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.2: Patience is the key to fishing.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.3: I caught a boot yesterday. Best trade of the week.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.4: The fish told me a secret: they hate boats.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.5: The one that got away had a saddle on it.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.6: I fish for compliments. And also fish.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.7: The river is glassy calm. Perfect weather for a quiet day of fishing.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.8: I know every good fishing spot.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.10: The ocean is calling my name.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.11: The lake is full of fish today, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.12: I need to mend my fishing net.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.13: Have you tried fishing at dawn, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.14: I caught a fish so big it told me its life story.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.15: My fishing rod has a name. It's called 'The Negotiator'.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.16: The fish are practically leaping into my boat. Who needs bait?
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.17: My boat is getting smaller with every catch. I think it's shrinking.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.18: Fishing relaxes me more than trading.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.20: I have been fishing since sunrise.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.21: Rain means the fish will be jumping tomorrow.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.22: I once fished under a full moon. Magical.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.3: I count sheep to sleep. It's exhausting.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.4: My sheep think they're dogs. I don't have the heart to tell them.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.5: Wool gathering is harder than it sounds.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.6: My sheep write poetry. It's baaaa-d.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.8: Wool keeps us warm at night.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.9: Counting sheep is how I sleep.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.10: My dogs are the best herders.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.12: The wool this season is incredibly soft. Everyone wants a piece of it.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.13: Mind your step, %s. The sheep leave presents.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.14: I taught a sheep to play fetch. Better than the dog.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.15: My sheep's wool is so soft it's suspicious.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.17: I have counted every sheep in this field.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.18: My best ewe gave birth to triplets. Proud uncle moment.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.19: My sheepdog rounds them up before I even ask. Best partner I've ever had.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.20: Have you seen my favorite sheep, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.21: The flock is restless tonight. Something is out there.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.22: Sheep hate the rain almost as much as I do.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.2: Feathers are hard to come by.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.3: I once shot an arrow and it came back. Still confused.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.4: My arrows are 99% accurate. The rest is for creative aiming.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.6: I fletched an arrow with my own hair. Big mistake.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.9: I fletch arrows for the guards.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.11: I'm testing a new arrow design, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.14: I fletched an arrow with a phoenix feather. Bad idea.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.15: My arrows are arrow-dinary. Get it?
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.17: A good arrow starts with a straight shaft.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.18: I need more flint for my arrows.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.20: The fletching on these arrows is my best work.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.21: Damp feathers ruin good arrows. Stay dry.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.1: I've read every scroll in this library. Some of them twice. Still learning.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.2: Have you read the ancient texts?
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.3: I once traded a book for an emerald. Worst deal of my life.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.4: Silence! I'm trying to read this picture book.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.5: I bookmarked a book about bookmarks. It was meta.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.6: Overdue fines are my retirement plan.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.9: I can lose myself in a good book for hours. Time just melts away.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.10: Emeralds come and go, but knowledge stays with you forever.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.12: I need to catalog the new arrivals.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.13: Have you returned that book yet, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.14: I wrote a book about writing books. A page-turner.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.15: Reading in the dark is how I got these glasses.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.16: This manuscript predates the village itself. Handle it with care.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.17: Please turn the pages gently. These books are older than both of us.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.18: I have read every book in this library.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.19: Trade me your stories and I'll trade you mine. Best currency there is.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.20: The enchantments in this book are powerful.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.21: I read by candlelight when the sun goes down.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.22: All this knowledge... lost to time...
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.3: I got lost drawing this map. The map is wrong.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.4: I once sold a map to a man's own house. He bought it.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.5: I mapped a map of maps. Completely pointless.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.6: My compass points to my bed. That's home.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.7: I'm charting a new route through the dark forest. Stay tuned.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.9: Every time I finish a map, I realize how little I've explored.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.10: I once mapped a cave for days.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.12: The world is vast and full of adventures.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.13: I could use your help exploring, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.14: I drew a map of my dinner. Complex topography.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.15: My maps are treasure enough. The treasure is friendship.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.16: I've mapped every hill within a day's walk. There's always more to chart.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.18: A blank map is an invitation, %s. The world is waiting for you.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.19: This map shows places I've never been. One day I'll visit them all.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.20: I need more paper for my maps.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.21: The ink is running on my maps. This weather is a disaster.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.22: So many places... I'll never chart them now...
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.1: Zombie bites are on the rise.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.3: I turned water into wine. Then I traded it for emeralds.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.4: The gods have a plan. Use gamemode creative
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.5: Holy water? I just use tap. Works most of the time.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.9: Healing takes patience and steady hands. I've been blessed with both.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.14: I tried to cast a spell. I sneezed. Still counts.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.15: My holy water is just sparkling water. Don't tell.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.16: My potions are simmering to perfection. A little more brewing does the trick.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.17: May the light guide your path, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.18: I have been studying ancient scriptures.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.19: This brew will cure any ailment.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.20: The stars have revealed great wisdom.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.21: I pray for dawn to come every night.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.22: May your soul... find peace in the next world...
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.2: A knight is nothing without his armor.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.3: I once made armor for a chicken. It looked fabulous.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.4: My armor is so strong, even I can't get out of it.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.5: I made chainmail armor using fire and without using creative mode.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.6: My armor is self-cleaning. Also self-denting.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.7: The forge is roaring today. Perfect temperature to shape steel.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.10: Every piece I forge has a piece of my soul in it. Real quality.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.11: That armor took me days to craft, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.12: I've kept the forge burning for three days straight. Lost track of time.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.13: I can upgrade your gear, %s. For a price.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.14: I made armor for a turtle. He was unstoppable.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.15: My armor has plot armor. It never fails.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.16: Try this on. I guarantee it'll stop an arrow or maybe a sword swing.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.17: I have forged the strongest shields.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.18: A helmet is the difference between coming home and not. Don't risk it.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.19: I sleep next to the forge. The warmth is better than any bed.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.21: You'll pay for the dent you just put in my armor!
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.22: My finest craft... couldn't save me...
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.1: A sharp blade is a friend.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.3: I made a sword so big I couldn't lift it. Great doorstop.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.4: I traded my best sword for a potato. No regrets.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.5: I forged a sword so sharp it cut the silence.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.6: I put a handle on a rock. Called it a hammer. Sold it.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.9: A sharp blade and a steady hand. That's all a warrior really needs.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.12: The clang of the anvil is my music.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.13: Come back if your sword breaks, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.14: I made a sword too sharp. It cut the future.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.15: My enemies fear me. Also my customers.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.16: This blade took me a full week to forge. Every curve is intentional.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.17: I once forged a sword for a hero. He named it after the village.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.19: The steel is high quality this batch.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.20: Try this axe, %s. It is perfectly balanced.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.21: I'll remember this when you come asking for a new sword!
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.22: Let this blade... be my final legacy...
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.1: The right tool for the right job.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.3: I made a pickaxe that mined faster than light. It broke immediately.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.4: My shovel has seen more adventures than most adventurers.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.5: Two shovels dig twice as fast. It's basic math.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.6: My axe is also my therapist. We split wood together.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.8: Nothing breaks my heart like a broken pickaxe. Bring it here, I'll fix it.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.9: I make tools for the miners.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.10: A good tool lasts a lifetime. I make sure every handle fits like a glove.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.11: This pickaxe will last a lifetime, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.12: Bring me your broken tools. I'll have them good as new in no time.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.13: Come see my new shovel design, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.14: I invented a hoe that hoes itself. Controversial.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.15: I put a handle on everything. Don't give me ideas.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.16: The right tool turns hours of work into minutes. Never settle for less.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.17: I have upgraded my pickaxe design.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.18: These shears are sharper than ever.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.19: You're only as good as your tools. Lucky for you, I make the best around.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.21: You break my tools, I break your knees. Metaphorically.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.22: Rust is the enemy of good tools. This weather is trouble.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.1: Fresh meat! Get your fresh meat!
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.3: I named every cow. It's a rough gig.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.4: My cleaver has its own fan club.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.5: I'm on a strict meat diet. I'm butchering it.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.6: My sausages won an award. No idea which one.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.9: I know every cut better than I know my neighbors. And I know them well.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.10: Cleaning the shop takes all day.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.12: The best cuts go to my best customers.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.13: I saved you a special piece, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.14: I named all the pigs. Dinner is awkward.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.15: My sausages are the talk of the town. Literally.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.16: Fresh delivery this morning, straight from the farm. You won't find better.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.17: I handpick every single cut myself. Quality over quantity, always.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.18: A good meal starts with quality ingredients.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.19: I have been working since before dawn.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.21: The cycle of life... catches up to us all...
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.22: I'm the one who does the cutting around here!
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.3: I once made boots for a horse. He wore them once.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.4: My leather is so soft it apologizes when you touch it.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.5: I made a leather hat for a pig. He looked distinguished.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.6: This leather is so soft it cries when cut.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.7: This hide came from a moose I tracked myself. Top quality, I promise you.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.9: Leatherworking is all about patience. You simply can't rush perfection.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.10: I treat every hide with care.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.11: This leather is from the finest cows, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.14: I made a leather jacket for a wolf. Pack leader.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.15: This hide has its own insurance. Premium.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.16: Feel this leather. Soft as silk but strong as iron. Special.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.17: I have tanned these hides for weeks.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.18: A good saddle makes riding comfortable.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.19: Good leather takes time and love. I've been curing this for weeks now.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.21: Water ruins leather. I should move my stock indoors.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.22: I work best when the village is quiet at night.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.3: My favorite rock is a boulder. We've been friends for years.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.4: I built a wall and it stayed. Unlike my last trade.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.5: I built a statue of myself. The pigeons love it.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.6: Granite is just rock with a superiority complex.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.7: Stone outlasts us all. Every wall I build will stand for generations.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.9: There's nothing more satisfying than watching a wall rise from nothing. Pure art.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.10: The quarry has provided for this village for generations. It never disappoints.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.11: This stone came from deep underground, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.12: Building takes patience. Stone takes more.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.13: Your house could use some repairs, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.14: I built a sandcastle once. Tide disagreed.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.15: I carry a brick for good luck. Heavy luck.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.17: I have been quarrying all week.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.18: A solid foundation is the most important part.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.19: Brick by brick, we build greatness.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.20: The arch design needs more support.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.21: Rain seeps into the cracks. Weakens the stone.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.22: Even the strongest stone... crumbles eventually...
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.3: Don't mind the zombies. They're just enthusiastic neighbors.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.4: I've been standing at this spot all day. Not a single trade yet.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.5: I dream of emeralds. Big, green, shiny emeralds.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.7: I still remember when you used to replace me with the villagers from Comes Alive.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.9: Nice day for trading, isn't it %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.10: I've been trading here since before you were born, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.11: The village council meets on Thursdays.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.12: Have you seen the iron golem? He's very dedicated.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.13: I heard there's a raid coming. Stay alert.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.14: My grandfather traded from this very spot. I'm carrying his legacy.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.15: The paths are full of holes. Someone tripped over my melons yesterday.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.16: The bell tower is looking splendid today.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.17: I wonder what's beyond the horizon.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.18: I've got the best deals in town, %s. Just say the word.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.20: I remember when this was all farmland.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.22: I like your armor, %s. Very shiny.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.23: The village gate is hanging by a thread. I patched it with spare planks.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.24: All this standing is doing a number on my back. Anything for the right trade.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.25: I applied to join Hermitcraft. They said I'm too villager.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.26: I've been saving up to add a second floor. The roof views must be incredible.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.27: Emeralds are getting scarcer every day. Trade now while you still can.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.29: The crops are growing nicely this season.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.30: I'm running low on stock. Let me check what's in the back.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.31: Have you seen the blacksmith's new anvil? It's massive.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.32: The well water tastes especially fresh today.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.33: I could use a break. Trading is exhausting.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.34: The village is growing faster than my wheat.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.35: Did you hear about the wandering trader? He tells tall tales.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.36: My chests are chaos. Wheat mixed with wool. Tools with seeds. I need help.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.37: Good morning, %s! Another day, another emerald.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.38: The bells are ringing. Must be a celebration.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.39: I tried to build a nether portal. Ended up with a very expensive fireplace.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.40: The librarian lent me a good book.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.41: I wouldn't want to face Coldified.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.42: I heard the wandering trader lost his llama again. Third time this month!
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.43: I saw a creeper doing yoga. Explosive relaxation..
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.44: My life is a speedrun. I'm just bad at it.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.46: aCookieGod said hi to me once. I framed the screenshot.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.47: I tried PvP once. Let's just say I'm better at trading.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.48: The Enderman stole my prize potato. I want compensation.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.49: My house is getting cramped with all my emeralds. First world villager problems.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.50: The well's been running dry. We might need to dig deeper soon.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.51: I've heard the blacksmith is looking for an apprentice.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.52: Have you seen the new fence around the sheep pen?
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.53: People buy that item a lot. Don't know why.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.54: I'll let it go for free, %s. The rest is on you.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.55: A fair trade for a fair customer, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.56: I've got plenty more where that came from.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.58: I hope the weather stays nice for the crops.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.59: Come back if you need anything else, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.60: I'd say you got the better end of that deal.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.64: The night is full of monsters. Be careful.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.72: What did I ever do to you, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.74: I'll give you a discount, just stop!
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.76: I hope this rain doesn't flood the crops.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.77: Rainy days are good for trading indoors.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.78: I used to love storms. Then lightning struck my house.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.80: This weather is bad for business.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.82: I should have... stayed in bed today...
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.83: Why... why would you... do this to me...?
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.84: The world... has grown so... cold...
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.85: Tell my son... that I loved him...
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.86: The sun is warm and the breeze is perfect. Days like this make trading worthwhile.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.87: I heard a creeper last night. Stayed awake for hours.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.88: Non-stop customers today. I've traded more in the last hour than all of last week.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.89: You look like you've been traveling far, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.90: The librarian lent me a fascinating book about ancient civilizations.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.91: Has anyone seen my favorite trading apron?
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.92: The village children were playing hide and seek near the bell tower.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.93: I hope the iron golem is getting enough rest.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.94: These old bones can't work as long as they used to.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.95: %s, you're always coming and going. Must be nice.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.96: I've been polishing my emeralds. They need to shine.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.97: The aroma from the bakery is drifting across the whole village. Delicious.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.98: I hope it snows this winter. The village looks magical under a white blanket.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.99: Be careful out there, %s. The world can be dangerous.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.100: A storm is coming. I can feel it in my joints.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.101: The villagers are saying you're quite the hero.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.102: I traded with a wandering trader yesterday. Interesting fellow.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.103: This door squeaks louder than my joints. I really need to oil it.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.104: You should try the stew at the butcher's. It's hearty.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.105: The sunset over the plains is beautiful tonight.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.106: I've been trading so long my legs fell asleep. Twice.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.107: Do you think the iron golem ever gets lonely out there?
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.108: The wandering trader's llamas spit at me again. Rude animals.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.109: I once traded a wooden shovel for a diamond. Best deal ever.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.110: The village well is the best place to hear gossip.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.111: I saw a baby zombie riding a chicken yesterday. Adorable nightmare.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.112: My emerald collection is coming along nicely.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.113: If I had a coin for every time someone asked for a discount...
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.114: I've memorized the blacksmith's hammer rhythm by now.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.115: I wonder what villagers in other biomes are doing right now.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.116: The bakery just put out fresh bread. I can smell it from here.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.117: I caught a fox stealing from the chicken coop this morning.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.118: The days are getting shorter. Winter is coming.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.119: Has anyone seen my good brown trading apron?
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.120: I organized my chests alphabetically. Yes, I'm that bored.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.121: I tried to become a witch. The application process is ridiculous.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.122: My life flashed before my eyes. It was mostly just trading.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.123: I applied to be the bell ringer. They said I lack the toll-erance.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.124: I told a joke to a nitwit villager. He burst out laughing a day later.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.125: I tried to count my emeralds. I'm still going. There are a lot.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.126: My therapist says I have trading anxiety. I traded him a carrot for the diagnosis.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.127: I tried to barter with a piglin once. He said my prices were insulting. Best compliment ever.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.128: The night is long, %s. Stay close to the torches.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.129: I can hear the zombies groaning outside. Lock your doors.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.130: The stars are beautiful tonight, but I'd rather be inside.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.131: I should have stocked up on more torches.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.132: The darkness brings out the worst in this world.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.133: Every creak in the night sounds like a creeper.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.134: I hope the iron golem is keeping watch tonight.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.135: These old eyes can't see in the dark like they used to.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.136: The night is when the village feels most fragile.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.137: I lit every street torch in the village myself. You're welcome.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.138: Rain again. The crops will be happy at least.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.139: I hate getting wet. My robes take forever to dry.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.140: Pitter-patter on the roof. Almost peaceful.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.141: All this rain is going to ruin my stock.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.142: Good day to stay indoors and recount my emeralds.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.143: The roads are turning into mud pits.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.144: I can hear water dripping. I should fix that roof leak.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.145: Rainy days mean fewer customers. Quiet, but bad for business.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.146: I saw a creeper standing in the rain. Looked somehow sad.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.147: The village looks clean after a good downpour.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.148: What was that for?! I was just standing here!
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.149: You'll regret that when you need a discount!
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.150: Is that how you treat your friendly neighborhood trader?!
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.151: I'll remember this, %s. I have a long memory.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.152: My ancestors are rolling in their graves!
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.153: I'm not a punching bag! Stop it!
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.154: I have connections with the iron golem. Think carefully.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.155: That's going to leave a mark! And a grudge!
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.156: If you wanted my attention, just say hello next time!
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.157: You're lucky I'm a pacifist by trade!
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.158: The village... will go on... without me...
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.159: My emeralds... are buried... under the old oak tree...
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.160: Tell the librarian... I never finished... that book...
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.161: I should have... retired to a mushroom island...
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.162: So this is... how my story ends...
